mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals rising consumer demand, which drives higher corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS 3-month change vs 60d forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a low baseline (0.25%), it indicates the end of an accommodative cycle, tightening financial conditions and raising discount rates, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 0.25%, average 20d forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll gains (PAYEMS rising) and declining unemployment (UNRATE falling) indicate a robust labor market; this dual signal of economic strength boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth * negative UNRATE change) has a coefficient not significantly different from zero in a predictive regression, or the composite signal yields average 60d returns < 0.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate post-recession recovery phase (12 months from NBER trough), rising industrial production (INDPRO) captures accelerating manufacturing activity, which leads to strong corporate profit growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (within 12 months of NBER trough)
failure_condition: In post-recession periods as defined, the rank correlation between INDPRO 3m change and 20d forward returns is not significantly positive (Spearman rho > 0 with p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and forces the Fed to tighten, reducing equity valuations; over a short 5-day horizon, monthly CPI surprises directly impact market pricing, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in CPIAUCSL shows zero or positive correlation with 5d forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE > 5.5%, mean 20d forward return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling housing starts (HOUST) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a downturn in the housing market and tighter financial conditions, which together depress economic activity and equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines >5% m/m and FEDFUNDS rises >50bp over 3 months, the hit rate for negative 20d returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a full employment regime (UNRATE below 4%), further payroll expansions (PAYEMS growth) lead to wage inflation pressures and anticipation of Fed tightening, which negatively impacts equities over the next 20 trading days as markets price in higher interest rates.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4%, the correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20d forward returns is not negative (Spearman rho >= 0) over any 5-year window.
